packages.overview: Shimla and Manali are two of Himachal Pradesh’s most beloved hill stations, offering a delightful mix of scenic beauty, colonial charm, and adventure. Shimla, once the summer capital of British India, enchants visitors with its historic buildings, bustling Mall Road, and panoramic views from spots like Kufri and Jakhoo Temple. Manali, set in the Kullu Valley, is a haven for nature lovers and thrill-seekers, with attractions like Solang Valley, Rohtang Pass, and Hidimba Temple. Whether you’re enjoying snowfall, shopping for local handicrafts, or simply soaking in the mountain air, Shimla and Manali promise a memorable and refreshing Himalayan getaway.

Kufri is famous for its Himalayan National Park, Poney and Yak Ride and One can see the endless Himalayan Panorama from Kufri, after lunch sight -seeing of various places in and around. Jakhoo Temple, Vice regal Lodge built in (1885), Christ Church built in (1844), The Gaiety Theatre- the first beauty contest of world was supposed to start (1887), The Scandal Point and The famous Shopping Plaza of the North The Mall. Overnight in Shimla.

Also visit the Kullu on the way to Manali. Arrive at Manali and check in the hotel, rest of the day is at leisure to visit the local markets overnight stay at hotel at Manali.

After a hearty breakfast, we set out for sightseeing of places in and around Manali, Like Hadimba temple, Manu temple, Vashisht Bath, (trek/Walk to Jogini Fall optional), Van Vihar and evening shopping at the local market. Overnight in Manali.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Manali – Solang Valley ","day_summary":"

After an early morning Breakfast,proceed to Solang Valley ( Snow Point ) Situated at 13 km northwest of Manali, particularly a pictorial spot presenting awesome scenery of snow clad mountains and glaciers. The most common sports at Solang Valley are parachuting, paragliding, skating and zorbing. Dinner at Hotel, Overnight Stay in Manali.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Manali to Chandigarh ","day_summary":"

After breakfast, check out from hotel and drive from Manali to Chandigarh

packages.overview:
Phuket – The Pearl of the Andaman

  • Thailand’s largest island, known for its beautiful beaches and vibrant nightlife.

  • Patong Beach: Famous for its lively nightlife, beach clubs, and shopping.

  • Phi Phi Islands: A breathtaking island getaway with crystal-clear waters.

  • Big Buddha & Wat Chalong: Must-visit cultural landmarks in Phuket.

  • James Bond Island: A scenic limestone rock formation, ideal for boat tours.

  • Phang Nga Bay: Stunning emerald waters, caves, and limestone karsts for kayaking.

Krabi – A Tropical Paradise

  • Known for its stunning limestone cliffs, turquoise waters, and serene beaches.

  • Railay Beach: A secluded paradise accessible only by boat.

  • Ao Nang Beach: A lively beachfront with restaurants, markets, and nightlife.

  • Four Islands Tour: Visit Phra Nang Cave Beach, Chicken Island, Poda Island & Tup Island.

  • Emerald Pool & Hot Springs: Natural hot water pools for relaxation.

  • Tiger Cave Temple: A cultural and scenic viewpoint after a rewarding climb.

In the evening, you'll be picked up from your hotel by a tour representative of Travelxploria—recognized as the best travel agency for Thailand—to embark on an unforgettable Phuket FantaSea tour. As one of the best tour operators in the region, we ensure a smooth and memorable journey as you are transported to this dazzling cultural theme park. Upon arrival, you’ll be immersed in a spectacular show that brings Thailand's rich heritage to life through captivating performances, intricate costumes, acrobatics, and breathtaking pyrotechnics. The magical atmosphere of Phuket FantaSea, with its unique blend of tradition and fantasy, promises an evening full of wonder and excitement. After experiencing the enchantment of the night, you'll be comfortably returned to your hotel, leaving you with lasting memories of Thailand’s vibrant cultural spirit.

After breakfast, you'll be picked up from your hotel lobby by a tour representative of Travelxploria—recognized as the best travel agency for Thailand—to begin your unforgettable Phi Phi Island adventure. Boarding a comfortable boat, you'll set sail with an experienced tour guide from one of the best tour operators in the region, who will enrich your journey with insights into the natural wonders and cultural history of the area.

Your tour begins with a scenic cruise toward Phi Phi Leh, the island famed for its breathtaking Maya Bay. Here, you'll be mesmerized by towering limestone cliffs, pristine white beaches, and crystal-clear waters—a perfect setting for a refreshing swim and stunning photographs. The captivating beauty of Phi Phi Leh sets the stage for the rest of your island-hopping experience.

Next, you'll venture to Phi Phi Don, the main island known for its vibrant local life and picturesque landscapes. Enjoy the dynamic energy of this island as you relax on its sandy shores and explore its natural charm. A special highlight of your journey is a stop at Monkey Beach on Phi Phi Don, where playful monkeys add a delightful touch to the tropical ambiance, making your experience even more memorable.

A delicious Thai lunch is served on board, allowing you to savor authentic local flavors while taking in the spectacular seascape. After a day filled with adventure, relaxation, and unforgettable moments, you'll be returned to your hotel, carrying cherished memories of the enchanting Phi Phi Islands—all thanks to the exceptional service provided by Travelxploria, the best travel agency for Thailand.

After breakfast, you'll be picked up from your hotel lobby by a tour representative of Travelxploria—recognized as one of the best travel agencies for Thailand—ensuring that your day-long adventure starts off smoothly. You'll then embark on a traditional long-tail boat / Speed Boat ride along Krabi’s stunning coastline, guided by an experienced tour guide from one of the best tour operators in the region, who will provide insightful commentary and ensure your comfort throughout the tour.

Your first destination is Phranang Cave Beach, where you can marvel at the dramatic limestone cliffs, explore hidden caves, and enjoy a refreshing dip in the crystal-clear waters. The journey continues to the Tup Islets, renowned for their striking rock formations and vibrant marine life, offering an ideal spot for snorkeling and capturing memorable photos—all under the watchful guidance of your experienced tour guide.

Next, you'll visit Chicken Island, aptly named for its unique shape resembling a chicken, where you can enjoy a leisurely swim and immerse yourself in the tropical ambiance. The adventure culminates at Koh Poda, a serene island boasting powdery white sands and turquoise waters, providing the perfect setting to relax and unwind. Along the way, a delicious Thai lunch is served, allowing you to savor local flavors while taking in the breathtaking views of the Andaman Sea.

After a day filled with exploration and relaxation, you'll be comfortably returned to your hotel, carrying with you unforgettable memories of Krabi’s natural beauty—all thanks to the impeccable service from Travelxploria, the best travel agency for Thailand, and the dedicated team of the best tour operator.

packages.overview: Over six unforgettable days, you’ll land in Port Blair and dive straight into history with a guided tour of the Cellular Jail followed by the evocative Light & Sound Show, then drift into island time as you stroll Corbyn’s Cove at sunset. On day two, catch an early ferry to Havelock Island, settle into your beachfront cottage, and spend the afternoon lazing on powdery Radhanagar Beach before sipping coconut water as the sky blushes at dusk. Day three brings underwater thrills—choose between snorkeling or scuba diving off Elephant Beach’s coral fringes—then unwind amid the ebony boulders of Kalapathar Beach. Midweek, you’ll ferry to tranquil Neil Island for two nights of serene exploration: cycle to the Natural Bridge, swim in Bharatpur’s clear shallows, and toast a pastel sunset at Laxmanpur Point. On day five, return to Port Blair to wander the ruins of Ross Island’s colonial relics and hunt handcrafted souvenirs at Sagarika Market. Finally, after a leisurely breakfast by the sea or one last dip at Corbyn’s Cove, you’ll be transferred to the airport, suitcase full of memories and a camera brimming with island magic

[{"city":"Port Blair","country":"India","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/India/Port Blair/1744972786362-AdobeStock_321207750-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Port Blair/1744972786862-AdobeStock_409203105-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Airport Pickup. Later Corbyns Cove Beach and Light and Sound Show at Cellular Jail","day_summary":"

Arrive at Port Blair in the morning/afternoon by flight, where our representative from Travelxploria will be waiting outside the airport, holding a placard with your name on it. Our best on-ground support staff will ensure a smooth transfer to your hotel, where you can rest and relax.

Later, accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria, we proceed to Corbyn’s Cove Beach. The coastal drive to this beach is breathtaking, with a stretch of around 4 km offering stunning views of the open sea on your left. You can take pit stops along the way to click pictures and soak in the beauty.

Afterward, we head to the iconic Cellular Jail to witness the enthralling Sound and Light Show, where the heroic saga of India’s freedom struggle is brought to life. The reenactment of the freedom fighters’ journey is both inspiring and emotional.

Post completion of this memorable experience, our Travelxploria team will drop you back at your hotel for an overnight stay.

Today, we depart for Havelock Island, the most breathtaking destination in the Andaman Islands, rated as the best island in India. Our representative from Travelxploria will ensure a smooth transfer as you board a ferry from Port Blair to Havelock Island.

Upon arrival, our best on-ground support staff will assist you with a seamless hotel check-in. After some relaxation, our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria will accompany you on a visit to Radhanagar Beach, one of Asia’s most stunning white-sand beaches. Take in the mesmerizing beauty, relax by the shore, and witness the breathtaking sunset.

After this unforgettable experience, our Travelxploria team will drop you back at your resort, where you can unwind and enjoy a peaceful evening.

Check out from the hotel in Havelock Island and board a premium cruise to Neil Island. Upon arrival, our representative from Travelxploria will be there to assist you with a smooth check-in at your resort. After some relaxation, we proceed to explore the top attractions of Neil Island.

We begin with a visit to the Natural Rock Formation, also known as Howrah Bridge. This stunning natural wonder, formed over the years by coral deposits, stands beautifully at Beach No. 2. Our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria will share fascinating insights about this geological marvel.

Next, we head to Laxmanpur Beach, a secluded paradise located 2 km north of Neil's jetty, perfect for unwinding and witnessing a breathtaking sunset. After this scenic experience, our best on-ground support staff from Travelxploria will ensure your comfortable return to the hotel for a restful night.

Ferry Timing: The ferry from Havelock to Neil Island is scheduled around 10:00 AM. Ferry schedules are subject to change, and our team will keep you updated accordingly.

Before departure, we visit Bharatpur Beach, the most popular beach in Shaheed Dweep, known for its pristine shoreline, water sports activities, and breathtaking coral views. Our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria will be there to guide you, ensuring you make the most of this visit with insights into the island’s beauty and adventure opportunities.

Upon arrival, our team will be there to welcome you and transfer you to your hotel. After relaxing and refreshing, we embark on a local shopping tour in Port Blair, where you can purchase souvenirs, handicrafts, and gifts for your loved ones.

Post shopping, our on-ground support staff will ensure your smooth drop-off at the hotel for an overnight stay.

Note: If your hotel is located far from the main city, our team will take you for shopping before hotel check-in to optimize your time.

For return ferry preferences from Neil to Port Blair, kindly update the Travelxploria holiday planner. Hotel checkout in Neil Island is usually at 9 AM.

packages.overview: With Travelxploria, from the moment you touch down in Port Blair, you’ll feel the island breeze as you unwind on the palm-fringed Corbyn’s Cove Beach and later witness the evocative Light & Sound Show at the historic Cellular Jail. A scenic cruise arranged by Travelxploria whisks you to Havelock Island, where you’ll lounge on the powdery sands of Radhanagar Beach until the sky blushes at sunset, then hop aboard a boat for complimentary snorkeling amid the coral gardens of Elephant Beach—complete with seamless hotel pickup and drop-off. Before you know it, you’re marveling at the ebony boulders and crystal waters of Kalapathar Beach, then gliding back to Port Blair by cruise, your hotel waiting to welcome you one last time. At journey’s end, Travelxploria ensures you’re transferred to the airport with a suitcase full of souvenirs and a heart full of island magic.

Later Corbyn’s Cove Beach and Light and Sound Show at Cellular Jail","day_summary":"

Arrive at Port Blair in the morning/afternoon by flight, where our representative from Travelxploria will be waiting outside the airport holding a placard with your name on it. You will be assisted by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria and escorted to your hotel by our best on-ground support staff of Travelxploria, ensuring a smooth and comfortable check-in. Take some time to rest and relax before we begin exploring the beauty of Port Blair.

Later, we proceed to Corbyn’s Cove Beach, a stunning coastal drive offering breathtaking views of the open sea on one side. This 4 km scenic stretch provides perfect spots for photography and short breaks to soak in the natural beauty.

Next, we head to the Cellular Jail to witness the enthralling Sound and Light Show, where the heroic saga of India's freedom fighters comes to life. Through dramatic narration and enactments, you will experience the struggle and sacrifices made for India’s independence.

Post completion of this immersive experience, our representative from Travelxploria will ensure a safe and comfortable transfer back to your hotel for an overnight stay.

Today, we embark on a journey to one of the most breathtaking destinations in the Andaman Islands, Havelock Island, renowned as the best island in India. Departing from Port Blair, you will board a ferry to Havelock Island, where our representative from Travelxploria will be eagerly waiting to welcome you upon arrival. You will then be escorted to your hotel by our best on-ground support staff of Travelxploria, ensuring a seamless check-in experience.

After some relaxation, our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria will accompany you to Radhanagar Beach, often recognized as one of Asia’s most spectacular beaches. Stroll along the pristine white sands, soak in the natural beauty, and enjoy the mesmerizing ambiance. You will stay at this beach until sunset, witnessing a truly magical sight as the sun dips below the horizon.

Post this unforgettable experience, our representative from Travelxploria will transfer you back to your resort, where you can relax and unwind, cherishing the beauty of Havelock Island.

packages.package_name: Pearls of the East: Andaman Sojourn
packages.overview: ​With Travelxploria, your Andaman adventure begins the moment you arrive in Port Blair, where you’ll feel the ocean breeze at Corbyn’s Cove Beach before stepping back in time at the Cellular Jail’s evocative Light & Sound Show. Next, set off on our exclusive Ross Island and North Bay Island excursion, exploring colonial-era ruins and snorkeling amid vibrant coral gardens. A scenic cruise then delivers you to Havelock Island, where the powdery sands of Radhanagar Beach invite you to unwind, followed by an exhilarating boat ride to Elephant Beach—complete with complimentary snorkeling in crystal-clear waters. Continuing your island hop, you’ll glide to Neil Island to bask on Bharatpur Beach’s shores and toast a pastel sunset at Laxmanpur Beach. Before returning to Port Blair, discover the majestic rock arches of Howrah Bridge, then cruise back for one last stroll through the island’s laid-back streets. Finally, Travelxploria ensures a smooth transfer to the airport, leaving you with sun-kissed memories and the promise to return.

Upon arrival at Veer Savarkar Airport, Port Blair, our Travelxploria representative will warmly welcome you and assist with your smooth transfer to the hotel, where you can rest and relax.

Later, our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria will accompany you to Corbyn’s Cove Beach, a breathtaking stretch of sandy shores lined with coconut palms, located just seven kilometers from Port Blair. It is an ideal spot for swimming and sunbathing.

Next, you will visit the historic Cellular Jail, also known as Kala Pani, where the British exiled political prisoners during colonial rule. Our guide will provide deep insights into its significance in India’s freedom struggle.

As the evening sets in, experience the captivating Light & Sound Show at Cellular Jail, where the best on-ground support staff of Travelxploria will ensure you have a hassle-free experience while witnessing the heroic saga of India’s independence come alive.

After an enriching day, you will be dropped back at your hotel for a comfortable overnight stay in Port Blair.

After breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will pick you up for an exciting excursion to Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Island (Ross Island). Once the capital of Port Blair during British and Japanese rule, this island showcases the ruins of colonial-era luxury. Our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria will take you through its historic landmarks, offering deep insights into its past significance.

Next, you will proceed to North Bay Island (Coral Island), also known as The Gateway to Port Blair. This picturesque destination is renowned for its vibrant coral reefs and thrilling water-based activities. You can indulge in Scuba Diving, Snorkeling, or a Glass Bottom Boat ride (at an additional cost) to witness the mesmerizing marine life. Throughout your journey, the best on-ground support staff of Travelxploria will ensure a seamless and enjoyable experience.

After an exhilarating day, you will return to your hotel for a restful overnight stay in Port Blair.

As per the ferry schedule, our Travelxploria representative will assist you in boarding the ferry from Port Blair jetty for a smooth transfer to Swaraj Dweep (Havelock Island). Famous for its pristine white sandy beaches and turquoise waters, Havelock Island is one of the most breathtaking destinations in the Andaman archipelago.

Upon arrival, our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria will accompany you to your hotel, where you will complete the check-in process and unwind.

In the afternoon, you will visit the world-renowned Radhanagar Beach (Beach No. 7), often regarded as the Best Beach in Asia. Within moments of stepping onto its powdery white sands and gazing at the endless horizon, you will understand why it holds such a prestigious title.

After a mesmerizing time at the beach, our best on-ground support staff from Travelxploria will ensure a comfortable transfer back to your hotel. Stay overnight in Havelock, soaking in the island’s tropical charm.

packages.overview: Arriving in Port Blair, you’ll unwind on the palm-fringed Corbyn’s Cove Beach before stepping into history at the Cellular Jail’s evocative Light & Sound Show. The next morning, a scenic ferry whisks you to Havelock Island, where the world-famous Radhanagar Beach invites you to lounge under swaying palms, followed by a vibrant boat ride to Elephant Beach with complimentary snorkeling among kaleidoscopic coral gardens. Your island hop continues to Neil Island, exploring the gentle shallows of Bharatpur Beach and watching the sun dip below the horizon at Laxmanpur. Before returning to Port Blair, marvel at the natural arches of Howrah Bridge, then end your journey with a seamless airport transfer—courtesy of Travelxploria—leaving you with sun-kissed memories to last a lifetime.

Upon arrival, our Travelxploria representative will greet you and ensure a seamless transfer to your hotel for check-in.

Begin your island exploration with a visit to Bharatpur Beach, a haven for water sports enthusiasts. Our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria will assist you in enjoying activities like snorkeling and a glass-bottom boat ride (available at an additional cost), offering you a mesmerizing view of the vibrant marine life.

Later in the afternoon, head to Laxmanpur Beach, renowned for its magnificent sunset views, where you can witness the sky painted in stunning hues.

As the day ends, our best on-ground support staff of Travelxploria will ensure your smooth transfer back to your hotel. The evening is free for you to relax or explore at your own pace. Stay overnight in Neil Island.

packages.overview:
• ​Report to Willington Island, Cochin, between 9 AM to 10:30 AM for boarding; set sail after lunch at 1 PM to Minicoy.
  • Arrive in Minicoy around 7 AM; transfer to the resort, enjoy a welcome drink, and relax for the rest of the day.
  • Explore Minicoy Island with a visit to the Tuna Canning Factory and Museum (optional).
  • Enjoy water sports at the resort, including scuba diving or snorkeling in Minicoy's coral reefs.
  • Visit the Minicoy Lighthouse and experience the rich culture of the Minicoy village.
  • After lunch, board the ship for the journey back to Cochin, departing at 6 PM.
  • Arrive in Cochin by 10 AM; disembark after breakfast. If traveling, ensure bookings are made after 2 PM.​

Guests are required to report to Willington Island, Cochin, between 9 AM to 10:30 AM for boarding the ship. Our TravelXploria representative will assist you with the check-in process, and the ship will set sail around 1 PM after lunch. Please note, on the day of departure, lunch will be included in your meal plan, continuing until the package concludes upon your arrival back in Cochin.

The ship will arrive at Minicoy Island around 7 AM, and our TravelXploria representative will assist you in transferring to the resort after breakfast on board. Upon arrival at the resort, our team will welcome you with a refreshing drink. After a brief introduction to the resort and its facilities, you can proceed to your room and enjoy the rest of the day at leisure.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Sightseeing of Minicoy Island","day_summary":"

Guests have the option to explore Minicoy Island during their stay. They can request the resort team to organize a sightseeing trip for them. The tour includes a visit to the Tuna Canning Factory and Museum.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Minicoy Island","day_summary":"

This day is dedicated exclusively to water sports at the resort. Guests can enjoy a variety of water activities available on the island. They have the opportunity to try scuba diving, exploring the vibrant underwater world of Minicoy's coral reefs. Alternatively, guests can opt for snorkeling, discovering the diverse marine life in the crystal-clear waters. It’s a perfect day for adventure and relaxation in the beautiful surroundings of Minicoy Island.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Sightseeing of Minicoy Island","day_summary":"

On this day, guests have the opportunity to explore more of Minicoy Island. They can request the resort team to arrange a visit to the iconic Minicoy Lighthouse, offering stunning views of the island. Additionally, guests can tour the local village of Minicoy, where they can immerse themselves in the island’s rich culture and traditions. This visit provides a unique opportunity to experience the lifestyle of the friendly Minicoy Islanders. It’s a perfect way to connect with the authentic charm of the island and learn about its history and heritage.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Departure to Cochin","day_summary":"

After lunch, our TravelXploria representative will transfer you to the ship for your return journey to Cochin. You will be dropped off at the ship’s boarding point, where you can settle in for the voyage. The ship is scheduled to depart from Minicoy around 6 PM. Please ensure that you are ready for departure at the designated time. We wish you a pleasant sailing experience as you make your way back to Cochin.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Kochi","country":"India","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/India/Kochi/1744974760670-AdobeStock_41502290.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Kochi/1744974762754-AdobeStock_109762413.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Kochi/1744974762970-AdobeStock_139324435.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Kochi/1744974763069-AdobeStock_208769608.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Kochi/1744974763191-AdobeStock_229260544.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Arrival Cochin","day_summary":"

This is the final day of your wonderful journey. After enjoying a delicious breakfast, guests will disembark at the Wharf in Cochin. Most of the voyage is completed by 10 AM, allowing ample time to explore the area. If you have a return flight or train on the same day, we recommend making your travel reservations for after 2 PM to allow for a comfortable transfer. We hope you’ve had a memorable experience, and we wish you a smooth journey ahead.

packages.overview:
Travelxploria representative will receive you at Agatti Airport and assist with a boat transfer to Kavaratti Island for check-in and relaxation.
Enjoy sightseeing around Kavaratti, visiting the local village, lighthouse, and serene beaches, followed by a relaxed evening.
Boat transfer back to Agatti, followed by sightseeing at South Beach, Eastern Jetty, and an afternoon boat trip to the beautiful Kalpitti Island.
Relax at Agatti Island or opt for an optional visit to the stunning Thinnakkara Island (payable at the property).
After breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will assist you with a timely transfer to Agatti Airport for your departure.

After a delightful breakfast, set out for a day of sightseeing and exploration. Begin by visiting a local village, where you can immerse yourself in the island’s simple yet vibrant way of life, observing the daily routines and traditions of the warm, friendly community. Next, proceed to the island’s iconic lighthouse, an impressive structure offering panoramic views of the stunning coastline and azure waters — a perfect spot for photography and soaking in the beauty of your surroundings. Continue your journey to one of the serene beaches, where you can stroll along the soft sands, enjoy the gentle sea breeze, and perhaps take a refreshing dip in the crystal-clear waters. As the day winds down, spend a relaxing evening at the beach, watching the sun set over the horizon. Later, return to your accommodation to unwind and relax, ending another wonderful day on this enchanting island.

On this day, our TravelXploria representative will transfer you to the boat jetty for your vessel journey to Agatti Island, a scenic sea transfer that takes approximately 2.5 hours. Upon arrival at Agatti jetty, our local assistant will warmly receive you and escort you to your homestay. After refreshing yourself, you will proceed for an Agatti Island sightseeing tour, including visits to the picturesque South Beach and the Eastern Jetty, the main point for ship embarkation and disembarkation. Following lunch, embark on an exciting afternoon excursion to Kalpitti Island, a small, uninhabited paradise located at the southern tip of Agatti. Accompanied by an experienced guide, you will take a private boat for a 30-minute journey across stunning waters. Enjoy the pristine sandy beaches, swim alongside turtles, and snorkel in the crystal-clear, shallow waters. After sightseeing on foot, return to Agatti in the evening to relax. Dinner will be served at 8:30 PM, followed by an overnight stay at Agatti.

packages.overview:
Munnar

Hill station known for tea gardens and cool climate

  • Tea Plantations: Endless stretches of lush green tea estates.

  • Eravikulam National Park: Home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr and Neelakurinji blooms.

  • Mattupetty Dam & Echo Point: Scenic picnic spots with boating and nature views.

  • Kundala Lake: Serene lake surrounded by rolling hills and pedal boating options.

  • Tea Museum: Insight into Munnar’s colonial tea heritage.

Alleppey

Famous for its tranquil backwaters and houseboat stays

  • Houseboat Cruise: Sail through the scenic backwaters, passing villages and paddy fields.

  • Alleppey Beach: Golden sands and a peaceful seaside vibe.

  • Backwater Canals: Ideal for a peaceful canoe ride or photo session.

  • Traditional Kerala Cuisine: Enjoy authentic meals served on a houseboat.

Varkala

Coastal town known for cliffside views and spiritual vibes

  • Varkala Cliff & Beach: Unique red cliffs overlooking the Arabian Sea, great for sunsets.

  • Janardanaswamy Temple: Ancient temple with spiritual significance.

  • Papanasam Beach: Believed to wash away sins – a peaceful spot for reflection.

  • Ayurvedic Spas: Rejuvenating massages and wellness treatments.

  • Cafes & Shopping: Laid-back cafes with sea views and local handicrafts along the cliff.

packages.overview:
• Kochi (Cochin) – Known as the Queen of the Arabian Sea, Kochi is a cultural melting pot where colonial charm meets coastal vibrance. Tourists are drawn to its historic sites like Fort Kochi, the Chinese Fishing Nets, and the Mattancherry Palace. The Jewish Synagogue and spice markets add to its multicultural appeal. Kochi also serves as the gateway to Kerala’s hill stations and backwaters.
  • Munnar – A dreamy hill station nestled in the Western Ghats, Munnar is famous for its endless tea plantations, cool climate, and misty hills. It's a haven for nature lovers and honeymooners, offering serene spots like Eravikulam National Park, Tea Museum, and Mattupetty Dam. Trekking, wildlife sightings, and scenic drives make Munnar a must-visit destination.
  • Alleppey (Alappuzha) – Often dubbed the Venice of the East, Alleppey is renowned for its tranquil backwaters and iconic houseboat experiences. A cruise through its lush waterways offers a glimpse into rural Kerala life. Tourists also enjoy Alleppey Beach, Ayurvedic wellness, and authentic Kerala cuisine served on board traditional kettuvallams.
  • Kanyakumari – Located at the southernmost tip of India, Kanyakumari is where the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and Indian Ocean meet. It's a spiritual and scenic destination known for the Vivekananda Rock Memorial, Thiruvalluvar Statue, and stunning sunrise/sunset views. Pilgrims, history buffs, and sea lovers alike are captivated by its unique coastal charm.

After check-in and freshening up, embark on a historical tour of Fort Kochi, visiting St. Francis Church, the iconic Chinese Fishing Nets, Jew Town, and the Paradesi Synagogue, along with the Mattancherry Palace (Dutch Palace) to admire its exquisite murals. Overnight stay in Kochi.

Spend your afternoon sailing through lush landscapes, spotting local fishermen and traditional Kerala homes, and unwind with a cup of masala chai while watching the golden sunset over the backwaters. Overnight stay on the houseboat.

After breakfast, drive to Kanyakumari (6-hour journey), where the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and Indian Ocean meet, creating a mesmerizing confluence of waters. Upon arrival, check into your beachside hotel before heading out to explore Kanyakumari’s iconic landmarks, including the Vivekananda Rock Memorial, a serene meditation site, the towering Thiruvalluvar Statue, and the Kanyakumari Temple (Bhagavathy Amman Temple), one of the revered 108 Shakti Peethas. As the day ends, witness the breathtaking sunset at Kanyakumari Beach, a truly magical experience. Overnight stay in Kanyakumari.

packages.overview: Your adventure begins with a warm welcome to Kochi, the Gateway to Kerala, where you'll explore the city’s vibrant markets, colonial architecture, and rich cultural heritage. From Kochi, journey to Munnar, where you'll be captivated by its scenic waterfalls and bustling local markets. In Munnar, discover the natural wonders of lush tea plantations and explore its wildlife in serene surroundings. Next, drift through the tranquil backwaters of Alleppey on a serene houseboat, immersing yourself in the beauty of Kerala’s waterways. Your journey continues with a visit to Kovalam Beach, where you can experience sunset serenity and unwind by the shore. Enjoy thrilling beach adventures and explore the coastal landscape before bidding farewell to Kerala’s enchanting beauty, taking home unforgettable memories of this tropical paradise.

Your journey begins with a warm welcome at Cochin Airport or railway station, where our Travelxploria representative ensures a seamless pick-up. Upon arrival, check in at your comfortable hotel and refresh before heading out to explore Kochi’s rich heritage. Start with a visit to the historic Mattancherry Palace, known for its stunning murals and royal artifacts. After a delightful lunch, continue to the Jewish Synagogue in Jew Town, a fascinating glimpse into Kochi’s multicultural past. In the evening, explore local markets, perfect for souvenirs and spices. Return to the hotel for a delicious dinner, then retire for the night.

After a good night’s sleep and a delicious breakfast, set out for an exciting tour of Munnar. Begin by exploring local markets, where you can indulge in window shopping and browse through souvenirs, spices, and handcrafted items. Next, immerse yourself in nature’s beauty with visits to the stunning Attukad, Valara, and Cheeyappara waterfalls, where the cascading waters and lush greenery create a mesmerizing experience. Spend time soaking in the tranquil surroundings before heading back to the hotel. Enjoy a sumptuous dinner, reflecting on the day’s adventures, and retire for the night, preparing for another beautiful day in Kerala.

After a refreshing night’s sleep and a hearty breakfast, set out for another exciting day in Munnar. Begin with a visit to the serene Mattupetty Lake and Dam, followed by a tour of the Tea Museum, where you can learn about Munnar’s rich tea heritage. Next, head to Kundale Lake, a picturesque spot perfect for relaxation. After lunch, explore the Eravikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, the last refuge of the endangered Nilgiri Tahr goat. If time permits, visit the Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary and admire the grandeur of Anamudi Hill, South India’s highest peak. Return to your hotel, enjoy a delicious dinner, and retire for the night.

After an early Kerala-style breakfast, embark on a scenic journey to Alleppey, often called the Venice of the East, with our Travelxploria representative ensuring a seamless transfer via a hired bus or taxi. Upon arrival, immerse yourself in the tranquil backwaters, where luxurious houseboats equipped with modern amenities glide through the scenic channels. If you choose, stay overnight in a houseboat, witnessing the still-working ancient Chinese fishing nets, lush paddy fields, swaying coconut groves, plantations, and historic temples. Alternatively, opt for a hotel stay, enjoy a relaxing boat ride, and end the day with a delectable Kerala-style dinner before retiring for the night.

After an early Kerala-style breakfast aboard your houseboat, check out and set off for the world-famous Kovalam Beach, with our Travelxploria representative ensuring a comfortable 160 km journey by bus or taxi. Upon arrival, check in at a beachfront hotel and take some time to unwind. After a delightful lunch, relax before heading to Kovalam Beach in the evening to witness a breathtaking sunset over the Arabian Sea. Stroll along the shore, explore the surrounding sights, or simply soak in the tranquil beach vibes. Return to the hotel, enjoy a delicious dinner, and retire for the night, recharging for the adventures ahead.

Start your day with a hearty breakfast before heading to Kovalam Beach for an exciting morning of water sports and fun activities like jet skiing, parasailing, and surfing. After a refreshing time at the beach, enjoy a delicious lunch before either relaxing at your hotel or setting off on an afternoon exploration of nearby coastal gems. Visit Hawah Beach, Lighthouse Beach, Samudra Beach, and the historic Padmanabhapuram Palace. If time permits, explore Veli Tourist Village and the stunning Varkala Beach. After a day filled with adventure and scenic beauty, return to the hotel, enjoy a delightful dinner, and retire for the night.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Bidding Farewell to Kerala’s Beauty","day_summary":"

Rise early and pack up completely before checking out of the hotel. If time permits, take a short 15-minute drive to the magnificent Shri Anantha Padmanabhaswamy Temple in Trivandrum, a revered landmark known for its architectural grandeur and spiritual significance. After seeking blessings and capturing a few final moments of Kerala’s charm, head towards the airport or railway station, with our Travelxploria representative ensuring a smooth transfer. Depart with beautiful memories of this incredible journey, forever cherished in your smartphone or camera, as you head home, reminiscing about Kerala’s enchanting landscapes, cultural wonders, and warm hospitality.

packages.overview: Your journey begins with the awe-inspiring Athirappilly Waterfalls, where you’ll experience the majesty of Kerala’s "Niagara" in all its glory. Continue your adventure with scenic exploration, discovering other beautiful waterfalls and the lush landscapes that surround them. In Munnar, immerse yourself in the region's natural beauty, with its rolling tea plantations, mist-covered hills, and wildlife-rich forests. Finally, bid farewell to Munnar, taking with you unforgettable memories of its serene beauty and tranquil atmosphere.

[{"city":"Cochin","country":"India","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Discovering the Majestic Athirappilly Waterfalls","day_summary":"

Your journey begins with your arrival at Cochin airport or railway station, where you'll be greeted by our Travelxploria representative for a smooth transfer. After a refreshing stop, head straight towards Athirapally, located 66 km away. The scenic drive takes you through Kerala's lush landscapes. Upon arrival, check in at your hotel and unwind. After a delicious lunch, venture out to explore the Athirappilly Waterfalls, also known as the \"Niagara of India.\" Take in the breathtaking beauty of the cascading water and tranquil surroundings. Afterward, return to your hotel to relax, and retire for the day after a sumptuous dinner, ready for more adventures ahead.

After a good night’s sleep and a scrumptious breakfast, head back to the majestic Athirappilly Waterfalls. Take a refreshing bath at the base of the waterfall, keeping safety in mind, and capture beautiful moments with your camera. Next, begin your sightseeing tour by visiting nearby waterfalls like Charpa and Vazhachal. Continue exploring the serene beauty of Vazhachal Forest Gardens and the peaceful Thumpurmuzhi Gardens. After a day filled with nature’s wonders, return to your hotel to relax and enjoy a well-deserved dinner. Retire for the night, preparing for your next adventure to Munnar, located 133 km away.

After a good night’s sleep and a delicious breakfast at the hotel, embark on a day of exploration in Munnar and its picturesque surroundings. Start your journey with a visit to the scenic Mattupetty Dam and Lake, followed by the tranquil Photo Point and Eco Point. Continue your adventure with stops at Kundala Dam and Lake. Then, head to the Eravikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr goat, or explore the stunning Attukad, Valara, and Cheeyyappara Waterfalls. In the evening, visit the local markets in Munnar for some window shopping and souvenirs. After a day of sightseeing, return to your hotel for a delicious dinner and retire for the night, ready for more discoveries the next day.

Depending on the timing of your flight or train to your hometown, head towards Cochin, located 130 km away. As you journey back, reflect on the stunning sights you’ve experienced. Upon arrival in Cochin, you'll have some time to relax or do a bit of sightseeing if your schedule permits. Finally, head home with memories of this unforgettable trip, all captured in your smartphone or camera, ready to cherish for years to come.

packages.overview: Your journey begins with your arrival in Kochi, the gateway to Kerala's vibrant culture, where you'll explore its rich heritage, historic sites, and bustling markets. From Kochi, travel to Munnar, where the magic of lush tea plantations and misty hills awaits, offering a serene escape into nature. A scenic drive takes you to Thekkady, where you'll immerse yourself in the wild beauty of the Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary, surrounded by lush forests and diverse wildlife. After Thekkady, you'll arrive in Alleppey, known for its tranquil backwaters, where you can unwind on a peaceful houseboat cruise. Your next stop is Varkala, a coastal gem offering beachside serenity, perfect for relaxation and enjoying the sea breeze. Finally, your Kerala adventure concludes with a departure, leaving you with unforgettable memories of this enchanting destination.

Upon arrival at Cochin International Airport, our Travelxploria representative will extend a warm welcome and ensure a comfortable transfer to your hotel in Fort Kochi. After a smooth check-in, embark on a cultural journey through Kochi’s historic streets, lined with Portuguese, Dutch, and British colonial landmarks. Explore the Mattancherry Palace’s royal murals, stroll through Jew Town’s spice-scented lanes, and admire the Chinese Fishing Nets at sunset. In the evening, witness a captivating Kathakali performance, showcasing Kerala’s rich artistic heritage. End your day with a delicious coastal dinner before unwinding in your cozy retreat.

– The Magic of Tea & Misty Hills","day_summary":"

After a delightful breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will ensure a smooth and comfortable transfer to Munnar, Kerala’s picturesque hill station. As you embark on this scenic 4-hour drive, enjoy mesmerizing views of rolling tea gardens and misty mountains. En route, pause to admire the Cheeyappara & Valara Waterfalls, capturing stunning cascades amidst lush greenery. Indulge in a fragrant spice plantation tour, where the scents of cardamom, vanilla, and pepper fill the air. Upon arrival, check into your charming resort and relax before exploring Munnar’s tea plantations, the Tata Tea Museum, and Echo Point’s unique acoustics. Unwind with a tranquil evening and a delicious dinner at your resort.

After a hearty breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will ensure a seamless transfer to Thekkady, a paradise for wildlife and nature lovers. Enjoy a scenic 3.5-hour drive through lush greenery, rolling hills, and spice-scented air before checking into your resort nestled amid spice plantations.In the afternoon, embark on a thrilling Periyar Lake boat safari, where you may witness elephants bathing by the shore, sambar deer grazing, otters playfully swimming, and a variety of exotic birds—with a stroke of luck, even the elusive Royal Bengal Tiger!

Later, take a guided spice plantation tour, immersing yourself in the fragrant world of cinnamon, cardamom, and pepper. End the day with a delicious dinner and relax at your serene resort.

After breakfast, embark on a scenic drive to Alleppey, the renowned \"Venice of the East,\" and check into a luxury hotel or resort overlooking the serene backwaters or the Arabian Sea. Experience the charm of Kerala’s waterways with a private traditional boat ride, gliding past lush paddy fields, swaying coconut palms, and quaint village life. Witness vibrant floating markets, fishermen at work, and the region’s iconic snake boats. Indulge in a delectable Kerala lunch featuring Karimeen fry, coconut-infused curries, and soft appams. As the evening unfolds, unwind at Alleppey Beach, where golden sands and a mesmerizing sunset create the perfect backdrop for a leisurely stroll or a quiet moment at a seaside café. Conclude your day with a delightful dinner before retreating to your cozy accommodation for a restful overnight stay.

After breakfast, embark on a scenic drive to Varkala, Kerala’s hidden coastal gem, and check into a luxurious resort or hotel with breathtaking views of the Arabian Sea. Spend your day unwinding at Varkala Beach and Papanasam Beach, where golden sands and rolling waves offer the perfect retreat. Immerse yourself in the town’s spiritual essence with a visit to the 2,000-year-old Janardanaswamy Temple, a revered pilgrimage site. For a serene escape, explore Kappil Beach, where the tranquil backwaters seamlessly merge with the sea, creating a picture-perfect setting. As the sun sets, relax with the soothing sounds of the waves before retreating to your comfortable accommodation for an overnight stay.

packages.overview: Discover the best of Himachal Pradesh with Travelxploria’s enchanting Manali & Dalhousie retreat. Begin your journey in Manali’s Kullu Valley, where snow-tipped peaks and whispering pine forests set the stage for heart-pounding adventures—soar over Solang Valley by paraglider, carve fresh tracks in the powder at Rohtang Pass, or ride the rapids of the Beas River. Then, drift into the colonial elegance of Dalhousie, where Travelxploria guides you through historic cantonment-style streets, lush meadows, and the “Mini Switzerland” of Khajjiar, dotted with crimson rhododendrons and serene lakes. Whether you’re chasing thrills in the mountains, strolling through British-era charm, or simply sipping chai as dusk paints the hills rose-gold, Travelxploria weaves every detail into a seamless, unforgettable Himalayan escape.

packages.overview: Discover the thrill and tranquility of the Himalayas with Travelxploria’s Manali & Kasol escape. Begin in Manali, where snow-dusted peaks and verdant pine forests set the stage for heart-pounding adventures—soar above Solang Valley on a paraglider, carve fresh tracks in winter at Rohtang Pass, or paddle through foaming rapids on the Beas River. Then we whisk you into the mystical Parvati Valley, centering in laid-back Kasol: wander riverside trails lined with cedar and apricot trees, sample Israeli-inspired cafés, and soak in the healing steam of the Manikaran hot springs. Throughout, Travelxploria handles every detail—from cozy mountain lodges to knowledgeable local guides—so you can lose yourself in Himalayan wonder, culture, and camaraderie.

After breakfast local sightseeing of Manali - “Hadimba Temple” built in 1553 with a superbly crafted pagoda roof, it is famous for its exquisitely carved doorway. “Club House” with comprehensive facilities that include roller skating, an auditorium, a billiards room, a library, a bar, and a restaurant makes a wonderful outing for the day. The excursionists can visit–Tibetian Monastery, Van Vihar, Manu Temple, Jogani Water Fall, and “Vashist Temple” are well known for their hot springs. There are old temples dedicated to the Saga Vashist and Lord Rama. Evening visits famous “Mall Road”. Overnight stay at a hotel in Manali.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":" Day excursion to Solang valley and Atal Tunnel","day_summary":"

Today we proceed for a full day sightseeing of – \"Atal Tunnel\", Solang-Valley, Nehru Kund, Gulaba, Kothi Village, Him Valley. Overnight stay at the hotel in Manali. While visiting Solang Valley one should also try hands-on adventure activities as it is one of the best places for adventures near Manali. You can enjoy activities like:- Paragliding, Cable Car Ropeway, Zorbing, Snow Scooter, Skiing, Horse Riding, and also can enjoy a short Trek to “Anjani Mahadev”.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Kasol","country":"India","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Moving towards Kasol ","day_summary":"

packages.overview:
1. Naini Lake – Experience the tranquil waters with a romantic boat ride, surrounded by the breathtaking Himalayan landscape.

  2. Sattal, Bhimtal & Naukuchiatal – Feel the cool mountain breeze while exploring these pristine, interconnected lakes filled with natural beauty.

  3. Hanuman Garhi – Seek divine blessings at this serene temple, offering a mesmerizing view of the sunrise and sunset.

  4. Naina Devi Temple – Witness the spiritual aura of this sacred temple, nestled in the heart of Nainital.

  5. Ranikhet – Stroll through the Queen’s Meadow, a town soaked in colonial charm and Himalayan beauty.

  6. Chaubatia Garden – Walk amidst the lush orchards, indulging in the fragrance of fresh apples and peaches.

  7. Golf Course – Admire the lush green fairways, set against the majestic Himalayan backdrop.

  8. Kausani – Experience the enchanting sunrise over the snow-capped peaks of Trishul and Nanda Devi.

  9. Gandhi Ji Ashram – Step into history at this peaceful retreat, where Mahatma Gandhi found inspiration for his writings.

  10. Army Museum – Discover the valiant history of the Kumaon Regiment, showcasing war memorabilia and heroic tales.

  11. Cave Garden – Wander through the mystical caves, feeling the thrill of adventure amid rock formations.

  12. Lover’s Point & Suicide Point – Marvel at the dramatic cliffs offering breathtaking panoramic views of the valley.

  13. Almora – Explore the cultural hub of Uttarakhand, known for its scenic beauty and heritage temples.

  14. Nainital Bazaar – Stroll through the vibrant markets, picking up handmade souvenirs and local crafts.

  15. Naini Lake Picnic – Enjoy a leisurely lakeside picnic, surrounded by the serene mountains and shimmering waters.

  16. Kainchi Dham – Find peace and spirituality at this famous ashram, a retreat for seekers and saints.

packages.overview:
1. Naini Lake – Glide across the serene waters, soaking in the breathtaking beauty of the surrounding hills.

  2. Hanumangarhi Temple – Seek divine blessings at this peaceful hilltop temple with mesmerizing sunset views.

  3. Bhimtal, Sattal & Naukuchiatal – Explore three picturesque lakes, each offering a unique charm and tranquility.

  4. Corbett National Park – Embark on an adventurous safari through dense forests, spotting majestic tigers and diverse wildlife.

  5. Jungle Safari – Experience the thrill of spotting elephants, leopards, and deer in their natural habitat.

Seek blessings at the Hanumangarhi Temple, a serene hilltop shrine offering breathtaking views. After a scenic four-hour drive, arrive at your destination and prepare for an exhilarating evening safari, where you might catch a glimpse of the majestic Royal Bengal Tiger, playful elephants, and vibrant birdlife. As the day ends, unwind at your hotel, savor a delicious dinner, and enjoy a restful night.

Spot majestic Royal Bengal Tigers, elusive leopards, playful elephants, and graceful deer roaming freely in their habitat. After this unforgettable adventure, return to your hotel, pack your bags, and cherish the memories of this enchanting journey. packages.overview:
• Uttarakhand: A serene Himalayan state known for its scenic beauty, lush hills, and spiritual charm.

  • Nainital: A popular hill station centered around the beautiful Naini Lake, offering boating, the Naina Devi Temple, Snow View Point, and Mall Road shopping.

  • Ranikhet: A peaceful retreat with pine-covered hills, lush meadows, Jhula Devi Temple, and panoramic Himalayan views, perfect for nature walks and relaxation.

  • Kausani: Known as the "Switzerland of India", this tranquil destination offers stunning views of Nanda Devi, tea gardens, and the Anasakti Ashram.

Your journey begins as you arrive at Kathgodam Railway Station, where our Travelxploria representative will be eagerly waiting to ensure a seamless pick-up and transfer to your next destination. Enjoy a scenic drive through winding mountain roads, taking in the fresh air and lush greenery. Upon arrival at your luxurious hotel, complete the check-in formalities and take some time to relax and refresh.

In the afternoon, embark on an enchanting sightseeing tour, visiting Cave Garden, Lover’s Point, Waterfall, Land’s End, Himalaya View Point, and Lake View Point—each offering breathtaking vistas. As the evening sets in, explore the vibrant Tibetan Market, perfect for shopping and experiencing the local culture. Unwind at your hotel for a peaceful overnight stay.

Begin your day with a picturesque drive to Ranikhet, a charming hill station known for its lush greenery and tranquil ambiance. Along the way, visit the sacred Kainchi Dham, a spiritual retreat nestled in the hills, followed by a serene stroll through Chaubatia Garden, famous for its fruit orchards and panoramic Himalayan views. Next, head to the renowned Golf Course, one of the highest in Asia, offering a perfect spot for photography and leisure. After a day of exploration, savor a delicious meal and retire to your cozy hotel for a peaceful overnight stay in Ranikhet.

to Kausani & Sightseeing","day_summary":"

Embark on a scenic drive to Kausani, a picturesque hill station often referred to as the “Switzerland of India,” nestled in the Kumaon region of Uttarakhand. As you ascend through winding mountain roads, enjoy breathtaking views of lush pine forests, terraced fields, and the majestic Himalayan peaks. Upon arrival, begin your exploration with a visit to the Anasakti Ashram, where Mahatma Gandhi once stayed and admired the tranquil surroundings. Next, witness the panoramic beauty of the snow-clad peaks like Nanda Devi and Trishul from the Kausani View Point. Visit the ancient Baijnath Temple, located by the Gomti River, known for its intricate stone carvings and spiritual significance. Take a peaceful stroll through tea gardens that blanket the slopes, offering a refreshing aroma and a chance to learn about tea processing. Kausani’s serene environment, fresh mountain air, and mesmerizing views promise a rejuvenating experience amidst nature’s purest form.

Our Travelxploria representative will ensure a smooth and comfortable transfer to Kathgodam Railway Station, where you will board your train back home. packages.overview:
Assam: A northeastern gem known for its tea gardens, wildlife, and rich cultural diversity.

Shillong: The “Scotland of the East,” known for its rolling hills, picturesque waterfalls, Shillong Peak, and serene lakes like Ward’s Lake.

Kaziranga: A UNESCO World Heritage site, home to the endangered Indian one-horned rhinoceros, offering safaris through its vast grasslands.

Guwahati: Assam’s largest city, famous for the Kamakhya Temple, Umananda Island, and the scenic Brahmaputra River.

packages.overview:
Assam & Meghalaya: A perfect blend of cultural heritage, lush greenery, and wildlife in Northeast India.

Guwahati: The gateway to Assam, known for the revered Kamakhya Temple, scenic Brahmaputra River cruises, and vibrant markets.

Shillong: The “Scotland of the East,” featuring scenic attractions like Shillong Peak, Elephant Falls, and Ward’s Lake.

Mawsynram: The wettest place on Earth, famous for its misty landscapes, Mawjymbuin Cave, and lush green surroundings.

Kaziranga: A UNESCO World Heritage site, home to the one-horned rhinoceros and thrilling jeep and elephant safaris.​

Embark on a captivating journey to Mawsynram, where the clouds kiss the earth and nature reigns supreme. Witness the spectacular Nohkalikai Falls, where water tumbles from towering cliffs, creating a mesmerizing plunge pool. After exploring this natural masterpiece, return to Shillong with your heart full of wonder, the sound of cascading waters still echoing in your soul.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Shillong Unveiled","day_summary":"

Ascend to Shillong Peak,with the views of the city and misty hills captivate your soul. Capture the magic of the landscape as the clouds play hide-and-seek. As the evening sets in, explore the vibrant Police Bazar—a treasure trove of local delicacies, souvenirs, and street-side energy and experience the heartbeat of Shillong, where nature and culture come together in perfect harmony.

Magic","day_summary":"

Begin your day with an enchanting elephant safari through Kaziranga’s dew-kissed grasslands. From your majestic point, witness the wilderness come alive—spotting rhinos grazing, herds of deer, and vibrant birdlife fluttering above. The soft morning sun and the rhythmic sway of your elephant create a serene connection with nature. After the adventure, indulge in a day of relaxation.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Shillong’s Last Goodbye","day_summary":"

Return to Shillong, the city where your journey filled with treasured memories. The winding roads and rolling hills seem to wave goodbye as you recount the thrills of Kaziranga and the wonders of Meghalaya. As you prepare for departure, savor the final moments of cool breezes and mountain charm. Though you leave, the melodies of waterfalls, forests, and the wilderness stay with you.

packages.overview:
Assam & Meghalaya: A perfect blend of natural beauty, wildlife, and cultural heritage in Northeast India.

Guwahati: The bustling gateway to Assam, known for the revered Kamakhya Temple, Brahmaputra River cruises, and Assam State Museum.

Shillong: The “Scotland of the East,” featuring scenic spots like Shillong Peak, Ward’s Lake, Elephant Falls, and a lively music and café culture.

Kaziranga: A UNESCO World Heritage site, home to the one-horned rhinoceros, elephants, and thrilling jeep and elephant safaris.

packages.overview:
• Arrival at Bagdogra Airport, drive to Phuentsholing (5 hrs), check-in, and evening free for local market exploration. Overnight in Phuentsholing.
  • After breakfast, drive to Thimphu (6 hrs), complete immigration formalities, and visit Wankha Waterfalls. Evening free for local market exploration. Overnight in Thimphu.
  • Local sightseeing in Thimphu including National Memorial Chorten, Buddha Dordenma, Changangkha Lhakhang, Motithang Takin Preserve, National Library, Painting School, Bhutan Textile Museum, Clock Tower, and Tashi Chho Dzong. Overnight in Thimphu.
  • Drive from Thimphu to Punakha via Dochula Pass, visit Punakha Dzong and Punakha Suspension Bridge. Continue to Paro for overnight stay.
  • Local sightseeing in Paro including Simtokha Dzong, National Museum, Ta Dzong, Rinpung Dzong, Drukgyal Dzong, Paro Airport View, and Kyichu Lhakhang. Overnight in Paro.
  • Drive from Paro to Phuentsholing (6 hrs), evening free for local market. Overnight in Phuentsholing.
  • After breakfast, depart for NJP Station/Bagdogra Airport with cherished Bhutan holiday memories.

The National Memorial Chorten, built in memory of the third Druk Gyalpo, is dedicated to World Peace and is popular among locals for various Buddhist religious festivals. Another notable site in Thimphu is the Buddha Dordenma Statue, a massive golden Buddha atop a hill with 125,000 smaller Buddhas hidden inside, offering panoramic views and often referred to as Buddha Point. Changangkha Lhakhang, a 12th-century temple, is known as the wish-fulfilling temple and offers stunning views of Thimphu city. For animal lovers, the Motithang Takin Preserve is a great spot to see Bhutan's national animal, the takin, in a preserved setting. The National Library, established in 1967, houses historic manuscripts, photos, and books, while the Institute for Zorig Chusum, or Painting School, teaches traditional arts and crafts. The Bhutan Textile Museum showcases the rich history of Bhutanese textiles and clothing. The Clock Tower Square, with its four-faced clock tower, is an architectural marvel and a popular gathering spot with many nearby restaurants and souvenir shops. Tashi Chho Dzong, a monastery located next to the Wang Chhu River, is famous for its annual Tsechu festival and was built in 1216 A.D. Overnight stay at a hotel in Thimphu.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Paro","country":"Bhutan","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618052142-AdobeStock_65598899.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618052760-AdobeStock_137774984.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618052855-AdobeStock_294984667.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618053002-AdobeStock_526226645.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618053279-AdobeStock_699351803.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618053406-AdobeStock_1070936357.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Thimpu – Punakha sightseeing to Paro","day_summary":"

Dochula Pass, located between Thimphu and Punakha at an elevation of 3100 meters, offers stunning views of the Eastern Himalayas. The pass is home to 108 stupas, and in winter, it is covered in snow, creating a beautiful scene. The Punakha Dzong, situated at the confluence of the Pho Chu (male) and Mo Chu (female) rivers, is considered one of the most beautiful dzongs in Bhutan. It is particularly accessible for first-timers, children, and elderly visitors, and offers a chance to explore the sacred history of this architectural marvel. Another highlight in Punakha is the Punakha Suspension Bridge, one of the oldest suspension bridges in the world, believed to have been built by the Buddhist monk Thangtong Gyalpo. This historic bridge, adorned with prayer flags, provides a perfect spot for birdwatching and nature photography, making it renowned for its picturesque appearance. Overnight stay at a hotel in Paro.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Thimphu","to":"Paro","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"3":[{"h_id":564,"h_name":"Silver Cloud Hotel","h_country":"Bhutan","h_city":"Paro","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"3","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Paro/Silver Cloud Hotel/1739868451834-Silver Cloud Hotel 1.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Paro/Silver Cloud Hotel/1739868451955-Silver Cloud Hotel 2.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Paro/Silver Cloud Hotel/1739868451999-Silver Cloud Hotel.jpg\"}]"}],"4":[{"h_id":566,"h_name":"Hotel Kaachi Grand","h_country":"Bhutan","h_city":"Paro","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Paro/Hotel Kaachi Grand/1739868660540-Hotel Kaachi Grand 1.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Paro/Hotel Kaachi Grand/1739868660611-Hotel Kaachi Grand 2.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Paro/Hotel Kaachi Grand/1739868660673-Hotel Kaachi Grand.jpg\"}]"}],"5":[{"h_id":568,"h_name":"Zhiwaling Heritage Hotel","h_country":"Bhutan","h_city":"Paro","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"5","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Paro/Zhiwaling Heritage Hotel/1739868803330-Zhiwaling Heritage Hotel 1.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Paro/Zhiwaling Heritage Hotel/1739868803455-Zhiwaling Heritage Hotel 2.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Paro/Zhiwaling Heritage Hotel/1739868803498-Zhiwaling Heritage Hotel.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Paro Local Sightseeing","day_summary":"

Simtokha Dzong: Built in 1629 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal, Simtokha Dzong is one of the oldest dzongs in Bhutan. Also known asSangak Zabdhon Phodrang, it holds great historical and religious significance. Constructed in the 17th century, this ancient fortress is a remarkable example of Bhutanese architecture and remains an important cultural landmark.

National Museum of Bhutan: Once an ancient watchtower, this museum now houses an extensive collection of Bhutanese artifacts. From traditional costumes and armor to weapons and daily life tools, the museum reflects the rich heritage and artistic traditions of Bhutan.

Ta Dzong: Originally built in 1649 as a watchtower to defend the Rinpung Dzong during times of war, Ta Dzong was later converted into Bhutan’s National Museum in 1968. It now serves as a cultural treasure trove, offering insights into the country’s past.

Rinpung Dzong: Also known as Paro Dzong, this majestic fortress was constructed in 1644 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal. It is an excellent example of traditional Bhutanese architecture and is one of Paro’s top tourist attractions.

Drukgyal Dzong: Known as the “Victorious Fortress,” Drukgyal Dzong is a historic Buddhist monastery and fortress. It commemorates Bhutan’s victory over Tibetan invaders and is considered one of the most beautiful archaeological sites in the country.

Paro Airport Viewpoint: Paro International Airport, Bhutan’s only international airport, is famous for its scenic beauty. Surrounded by dramatic mountains and lush valleys, it is considered one of the most stunning airports in the world and a must-visit spot for travelers in Paro.

Kyichu Lhakhang: Also called Kyerchu Lhakhang, this sacred temple is one of the oldest and most revered pilgrimage sites in Bhutan. It is part of a network of 108 temples built by a Tibetan king and is a cherished spiritual destination known for its serene beauty.

packages.overview:
• Arrive at Bagdogra, complete immigration at Phuentsholing, and enjoy a scenic drive to Thimphu with enroute sightseeing including Milarepa Tower and Tashi Choedzong.
  • ​Excursion to Punakha with visits to Dochula Pass, 108 Chortens, Punakha Dzong, Suspension Bridge, and Chimi Lhakhang, then return to Thimphu.
  • ​Thimphu sightseeing including Buddha Viewpoint, Simply Bhutan, Wangdi Tse Hike, then transfer to Paro with a stop at Tachog Monastery.
  • Hike to the iconic Tiger’s Nest Monastery, visit a local beer factory, and enjoy the Paro Airport viewpoint.
  • ​Explore Kyichu Lhakhang and Rinpung Dzong, then drive to Phuentsholing for exit formalities and departure from Jaigaon.​

[{"city":"Thimphu","country":"Bhutan","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Thimphu/1744618074051-AdobeStock_178486151.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Thimphu/1744618074433-AdobeStock_359113866.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Thimphu/1744618074881-AdobeStock_483696574.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Thimphu/1744618075034-AdobeStock_797771545.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Arrival at Bagdogra and transfer to Thimphu","day_summary":"

Upon arrival at Bagdogra, our representative will warmly welcome you and assist with the immigration formalities (depending on the arrival timing). Afterward, you'll drive to Phuentsholing, then continue the scenic journey to Thimphu via Kharbandi, with a stop at Milarepa Tower. Along the way, enjoy the picturesque towns of Gedu, Chukha, and Chuzom. Once in Thimphu, check into your hotel and take in the view of Tashi Choedzong before spending the evening at leisure. Your overnight stay will be in Thimphu.

After enjoying breakfast at the hotel, begin your scenic drive to Punakha. En route, stop at the stunning Dochula Pass, located at an altitude of 3,150 meters, to take in panoramic Himalayan views and visit the iconic 108 Druk Wangyel Chortens. Continue onward to explore the majestic Punakha Dzong, a masterpiece of Bhutanese architecture nestled between two rivers. Take a walk across Bhutan’s longest suspension bridge, offering breathtaking valley views, followed by a short hike to Chimi Lhakhang, also known as the Temple of Fertility. Later, drive back to Thimphu where you can enjoy a leisurely evening exploring the town. Overnight stay will be in Thimphu.

packages.overview:
• ​Arrival at Bagdogra Airport and transfer to Phuentsholing with scenic views of tea gardens.​
  • Immigration formalities and drive to Thimphu with en route photo stops and local sightseeing.​
  • Excursion to Punakha with visits to Dochula Pass, Suspension Bridge, Chimi Lhakhang & Punakha Dzong.​
  • Thimphu local sightseeing including Memorial Chorten, Buddha Dordenma, and Changangkha Lhakhang; drive to Paro.​
  • Hike to the iconic Tiger’s Nest Monastery (Paro Taktsang).
  • Paro sightseeing covering Simtokha Dzong, National Museum, Rinpung Dzong, Drukgyal Dzong, and Kyichu Lhakhang; return to Phuentsholing.
  • Departure from Phuentsholing to Bagdogra Airport with fond memories of Bhutan.​

to Thimphu drive","day_summary":"

After breakfast, complete the immigration formalities and proceed to Thimphu, the capital of Bhutan, via Gedu, located about 9,000 feet above sea level, with a scenic view of Chukha Dam. En route, enjoy a photo stop at Wankha Waterfalls. Upon arrival in Thimphu, check in to the hotel and explore the local market in the evening. Visit the Tashichho Dzong, a historic monastery by the Wang Chhu River, also known as Thimphu Dzong, which hosts the annual three-day Tsechu festival and dates back to 1216 A.D. In the evening, stroll around Clock Tower Square, a popular architectural landmark with four clock faces, surrounded by restaurants and souvenir shops. Overnight stay at a hotel in Thimphu.

After enjoying breakfast at the hotel, begin your scenic drive to Punakha. En route, stop at the stunning Dochula Pass, located at an altitude of 3,150 meters, to take in panoramic Himalayan views and visit the iconic 108 Druk Wangyel Chortens. Continue onward to explore the majestic Punakha Dzong, a masterpiece of Bhutanese architecture nestled between two rivers. Take a walk across Bhutan’s longest suspension bridge, offering breathtaking valley views, followed by a short hike to Chimi Lhakhang, also known as the Temple of Fertility. Later, drive back to Thimphu where you can enjoy a leisurely evening exploring the town. Overnight stay will be in Thimphu.

The National Memorial Chorten in Thimphu was built in memory of the third Druk Gyalpo (King) of Bhutan and is dedicated to world peace. It is a revered site for locals and hosts several major Buddhist religious festivals, making it one of the most significant places to visit in Thimphu.

The Buddha Dordenma Statue, perched on a hilltop, is an iconic landmark in Thimphu. This grand golden statue of Buddha sits above a large meditation hall and houses 125,000 smaller Buddha statues inside, making it home to more Buddhas than the population of the city itself. It is commonly referred to as Buddha Point.

Another must-visit site is Changangkha Lhakhang, one of the oldest temples in Thimphu, dating back to the 12th century. Known as the \"wish-fulfilling temple,\" it holds deep religious significance. The temple also offers a stunning panoramic view of Thimphu city.

","a_weekday":"[]","a_offday":"[]","status":"A","supplier":"","relative_city":"[\"Thimphu\"]","inserted_id":"[731]","edited_by_id":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","imageurl":"","images":"[{\"path\":\"/Activity/Paro/Thimphu local sightseeing/1745915470405-Thimphu local sightseeing 2.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Activity/Paro/Thimphu local sightseeing/1745915470572-Thimphu local sightseeing.jfif\"},{\"path\":\"/Activity/Paro/Thimphu local sightseeing/1745915470574-Thimphu local sightseeing 1.jpg\"}]"}}]},{"day_title":"Paro tiger nest hiking","day_summary":"

Paro Taktsang, also known as the Tiger’s Nest Monastery, is one of the most sacred Vajrayana Buddhist sites in the Himalayas, dramatically perched on a cliffside in the upper Paro Valley of Bhutan. It is one of the thirteen legendary “Tiger’s Nest” caves across historical Tibet where Guru Padmasambhava is believed to have meditated and taught Vajrayana Buddhism.

The present-day monastery complex was constructed in 1692 around the Taktsang Senge Samdup cave, where Guru Padmasambhava, along with his disciples including Yeshe Tsogyal, engaged in deep spiritual practice before leaving Tibet in the early 9th century. Revered as the founder of Vajrayana Buddhism in Bhutan, Padmasambhava is also regarded as the country's spiritual protector.

Today, Paro Taktsang stands as the most iconic of these sacred caves, drawing pilgrims and travelers alike. After visiting this revered site, enjoy an overnight stay at your hotel in Paro.

","a_weekday":"[]","a_offday":"[]","status":"A","supplier":"","relative_city":"[\"Paro\"]","inserted_id":"[732]","edited_by_id":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","imageurl":"","images":"[{\"path\":\"/Activity/Paro/Paro tiger nest hiking/1745915187060-Paro tiger nest hiking 1.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Activity/Paro/Paro tiger nest hiking/1745915187214-Paro tiger nest hiking.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Activity/Paro/Paro tiger nest hiking/1745915187223-Paro tiger nest hiking 2.jpg\"}]"}}]}]},{"city":"Phuentsholing","country":"Bhutan","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Paro Sightseeing – Back to Phuentsholing","day_summary":"

Simtokha Dzong: Built in 1629 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal, Simtokha Dzong is one of the oldest dzongs in Bhutan. Also known as Sangak Zabdhon Phodrang, it holds great historical and religious significance. Constructed in the 17th century, this ancient fortress is a remarkable example of Bhutanese architecture and remains an important cultural landmark.

National Museum of Bhutan: Once an ancient watchtower, this museum now houses an extensive collection of Bhutanese artifacts. From traditional costumes and armor to weapons and daily life tools, the museum reflects the rich heritage and artistic traditions of Bhutan.

Ta Dzong: Originally built in 1649 as a watchtower to defend the Rinpung Dzong during times of war, Ta Dzong was later converted into Bhutan’s National Museum in 1968. It now serves as a cultural treasure trove, offering insights into the country’s past.

Rinpung Dzong: Also known as Paro Dzong, this majestic fortress was constructed in 1644 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal. It is an excellent example of traditional Bhutanese architecture and is one of Paro’s top tourist attractions.

Drukgyal Dzong: Known as the “Victorious Fortress,” Drukgyal Dzong is a historic Buddhist monastery and fortress. It commemorates Bhutan’s victory over Tibetan invaders and is considered one of the most beautiful archaeological sites in the country.

Paro Airport Viewpoint: Paro International Airport, Bhutan’s only international airport, is famous for its scenic beauty. Surrounded by dramatic mountains and lush valleys, it is considered one of the most stunning airports in the world and a must-visit spot for travelers in Paro.

Kyichu Lhakhang: Also called Kyerchu Lhakhang, this sacred temple is one of the oldest and most revered pilgrimage sites in Bhutan. It is part of a network of 108 temples built by a Tibetan king and is a cherished spiritual destination known for its serene beauty.

packages.overview: Embark on an unforgettable Singapore adventure with Travelxploria, where every moment pulsates with excitement and wonder. From a thrilling Night Safari that brings you face-to-face with elusive nocturnal wildlife to a panoramic ascent atop Marina Bay Sands SkyPark, our comprehensive sightseeing tour showcases the city’s most iconic landmarks. Wander through the futuristic Supertree Groves and misty peaks of the Cloud Forest at Gardens by the Bay, then dive into movie magic at Universal Studios with exhilarating rides and live entertainment. Feel your heart race on Sentosa Island—whether you’re marveling at the Wings of Time light show, savoring beachside relaxation at Siloso, or riding the waves of Adventure Cove Waterpark. Explore the vibrant streets of Chinatown and Little India, indulge in local and international culinary delights, and capture the perfect snapshot at Merlion Park before immersing yourself in the wonders of the S.E.A. Aquarium. With luxurious transfers and handpicked shopping and cultural experiences, Travelxploria ensures your journey is as seamless as it is sensational.

With Travelxploria’s best on-ground support, your Singapore journey begins with comfort, ease, and unforgettable first impressions.
